verses.latin_vulgate: Agrippa vero ad Paulum ait: Permittitur tibi loqui pro temetipso. Tunc Paulus extenta manu cœpit rationem reddere:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγρίππας δὲ πρὸς τὸν παῦλον ἔφη ἐπιτρέπεταί σοι ὑπὲρ σεαυτοῦ λέγειν τότε ὁ παῦλος ἀπελογεῖτο ἐκτείνας τὴν χεῖρα
verses.king_james: Then Agrippa said unto Paul, Thou art permitted to speak for thyself. Then Paul stretched forth the hand, and answered for himself:

verses.latin_vulgate: De omnibus quibus accusor a Judæis, rex Agrippa, æstimo me beatum apud te cum sim defensurus me hodie,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ πάντων ὧν ἐγκαλοῦμαι ὑπὸ ἰουδαίων βασιλεῦ ἀγρίππα ἥγημαι ἐμαυτὸν μακάριον μέλλων ἀπολογεῖσθαι ἐπὶ σοῦ σήμερον
verses.king_james: I think myself happy, king Agrippa, because I shall answer for myself this day before thee touching all the things whereof I am accused of the Jews:

verses.latin_vulgate: maxime te sciente omnia, et quæ apud Judæos sunt consuetudines et quæstiones: propter quod obsecro patienter me audias.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μάλιστα γνώστην ὄντα σε πάντων τῶν κατὰ ἰουδαίους ἐθῶν τε καὶ ζητημάτων διὸ δέομαι σου, μακροθύμως ἀκοῦσαί μου
verses.king_james: Especially *because I know* thee to be expert in all customs and questions which are among the Jews: wherefore I beseech thee to hear me patiently.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quidem vitam meam a juventute, quæ ab initio fuit in gente mea in Jerosolymis, noverunt omnes Judæi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὴν μὲν οὖν βίωσίν μου τὴν ἐκ νεότητος τὴν ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς γενομένην ἐν τῷ ἔθνει μου ἔν ἱεροσολύμοις ἴσασιν πάντες οἱ ἰουδαῖοι
verses.king_james: My manner of life from my youth, which was at the first among mine own nation at Jerusalem, know all the Jews;

verses.latin_vulgate: præscientes me ab initio (si velint testimonium perhibere) quoniam secundum certissimam sectam nostræ religionis vixi pharisæus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προγινώσκοντές με ἄνωθεν ἐὰν θέλωσιν μαρτυρεῖν ὅτι κατὰ τὴν ἀκριβεστάτην αἵρεσιν τῆς ἡμετέρας θρησκείας ἔζησα φαρισαῖος
verses.king_james: Which knew me from the beginning, if they would testify, that after the most straitest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc, in spe quæ ad patres nostros repromissionis facta est a Deo, sto judicio subjectus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ νῦν ἐπ᾽ ἐλπίδι τῆς πρὸς τοὺς πατέρας ἐπαγγελίας γενομένης ὑπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ ἕστηκα κρινόμενος
verses.king_james: And now I stand and am judged for the hope of the promise made of God unto our fathers:

verses.latin_vulgate: in quam duodecim tribus nostræ nocte ac die deservientes, sperant devenire. De qua spe accusor a Judæis, rex.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς ἣν τὸ δωδεκάφυλον ἡμῶν ἐν ἐκτενείᾳ νύκτα καὶ ἡμέραν λατρεῦον ἐλπίζει καταντῆσαι περὶ ἡς ἐλπίδος ἐγκαλοῦμαι βασιλεῦ ἀγρίππα ὑπὸ τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: Unto which *promise* our twelve tribes, instantly serving *God* day and night, hope to come. For which hope's sake, king Agrippa, i am accused of the Jews.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid incredibile judicatur apud vos, si Deus mortuos suscitat?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί ἄπιστον κρίνεται παρ᾽ ὑμῖν εἰ ὁ θεὸς νεκροὺς ἐγείρει
verses.king_james: Why should it be thought a thing incredible with you, that God should raise the dead?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego quidem existimaveram me adversus nomen Jesu Nazareni debere multa contraria agere,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ μὲν οὖν ἔδοξα ἐμαυτῷ πρὸς τὸ ὄνομα ἰησοῦ τοῦ ναζωραίου δεῖν πολλὰ ἐναντία πρᾶξαι
verses.king_james: I verily thought with myself, that I ought to do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth.

verses.latin_vulgate: quod et feci Jerosolymis, et multos sanctorum ego in carceribus inclusi, a principibus sacerdotum potestate accepta: et cum occiderentur, detuli sententiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃ καὶ ἐποίησα ἐν ἱεροσολύμοις καὶ πολλούς τῶν ἁγίων ἐγὼ φυλακαῖς κατέκλεισα τὴν παρὰ τῶν ἀρχιερέων ἐξουσίαν λαβών ἀναιρουμένων τε αὐτῶν κατήνεγκα ψῆφον
verses.king_james: Which thing I also did in Jerusalem: and many of the saints did I shut up in prison, having received authority from the chief priests; and when they were put to death, I gave my voice against *them*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et per omnes synagogas frequenter puniens eos, compellebam blasphemare: et amplius insaniens in eos, persequebar usque in exteras civitates.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κατὰ πάσας τὰς συναγωγὰς πολλάκις τιμωρῶν αὐτοὺς ἠνάγκαζον βλασφημεῖν περισσῶς τε ἐμμαινόμενος αὐτοῖς ἐδίωκον ἕως καὶ εἰς τὰς ἔξω πόλεις
verses.king_james: And I punished them oft in every synagogue, and compelled *them* to blaspheme; and being exceedingly mad against them, I persecuted *them* even unto strange cities.

verses.latin_vulgate: In quibus dum irem Damascum cum potestate et permissu principum sacerdotum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν οἷς καὶ πορευόμενος εἰς τὴν δαμασκὸν μετ᾽ ἐξουσίας καὶ ἐπιτροπῆς τῆς παρὰ τῶν ἀρχιερέων
verses.king_james: Whereupon as I went to Damascus with authority and commission from the chief priests,

verses.latin_vulgate: die media in via vidi, rex, de cælo supra splendorem solis circumfulsisse me lumen, et eos qui mecum simul erant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμέρας μέσης κατὰ τὴν ὁδὸν εἶδον βασιλεῦ οὐρανόθεν ὑπὲρ τὴν λαμπρότητα τοῦ ἡλίου περιλάμψαν με φῶς καὶ τοὺς σὺν ἐμοὶ πορευομένους
verses.king_james: At midday, O king, I saw in the way a light from heaven, above the brightness of the sun, shining round about me and them which journeyed with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnesque nos cum decidissemus in terram, audivi vocem loquentem mihi hebraica lingua: Saule, Saule, quid me persequeris? durum est tibi contra stimulum calcitrare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντων δέ καταπεσόντων ἡμῶν εἰς τὴν γῆν ἤκουσα φωνὴν λαλοῦσαν πρός με καὶ λέγουσαν τῇ ἑβραΐδι διαλέκτῳ σαοὺλ σαούλ τί με διώκεις σκληρόν σοι πρὸς κέντρα λακτίζειν
verses.king_james: And when we were all fallen to the earth, I heard a voice speaking unto me, and saying in the Hebrew tongue, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? *it is* hard for thee to kick against the pricks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem dixi: Quis es, domine? Dominus autem dixit: Ego sum Jesus, quem tu persequeris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ δὲ εἶπον, τίς εἶ κύριε ὁ δὲ εἶπεν ἐγώ εἰμι ἰησοῦς ὃν σὺ διώκεις
verses.king_james: And I said, Who art thou, Lord? And he said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed exsurge, et sta super pedes tuos: ad hoc enim apparui tibi, ut constituam te ministrum, et testem eorum quæ vidisti, et eorum quibus apparebo tibi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ ἀνάστηθι καὶ στῆθι ἐπὶ τοὺς πόδας σου εἰς τοῦτο γὰρ ὤφθην σοι προχειρίσασθαί σε ὑπηρέτην καὶ μάρτυρα ὧν τε εἶδές ὧν τε ὀφθήσομαί σοι
verses.king_james: But rise, and stand upon thy feet: for I have appeared unto thee for this purpose, to make thee a minister and a witness both of these things which thou hast seen, and of those things in the which I will appear unto thee;

verses.latin_vulgate: eripiens te de populo et gentibus, in quas nunc ego mitto te,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξαιρούμενός σε ἐκ τοῦ λαοῦ καὶ τῶν ἐθνῶν εἰς οὓς νῦν σε ἀποστέλλω
verses.king_james: Delivering thee from the people, and *from* the Gentiles, unto whom now I send thee,

verses.latin_vulgate: aperire oculos eorum, ut convertantur a tenebris ad lucem, et de potestate Satanæ ad Deum, ut accipiant remissionem peccatorum, et sortem inter sanctos, per fidem quæ est in me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνοῖξαι ὀφθαλμοὺς αὐτῶν τοῦ ἐπιστρέψαι ἀπὸ σκότους εἰς φῶς καὶ τῆς ἐξουσίας τοῦ σατανᾶ ἐπὶ τὸν θεόν τοῦ λαβεῖν αὐτοὺς ἄφεσιν ἁμαρτιῶν καὶ κλῆρον ἐν τοῖς ἡγιασμένοις πίστει τῇ εἰς ἐμέ
verses.king_james: To open their eyes, *and* to turn *them* from darkness to light, and *from* the power of Satan unto God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins, and inheritance among them which are sanctified by faith that is in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unde, rex Agrippa, non fui incredulus cælesti visioni:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅθεν βασιλεῦ ἀγρίππα οὐκ ἐγενόμην ἀπειθὴς τῇ οὐρανίῳ ὀπτασίᾳ
verses.king_james: Whereupon, O king Agrippa, I was not disobedient unto the heavenly vision:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed his qui sunt Damasci primum, et Jerosolymis, et in omnem regionem Judææ, et gentibus, annuntiabam, ut pœnitentiam agerent, et converterentur ad Deum, digna pœnitentiæ opera facientes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ τοῖς ἐν δαμασκῷ πρῶτόν καὶ ἱεροσολύμοις εἰς πᾶσάν τε τὴν χώραν τῆς ἰουδαίας καὶ τοῖς ἔθνεσιν ἀπαγγέλλων μετανοεῖν καὶ ἐπιστρέφειν ἐπὶ τὸν θεόν ἄξια τῆς μετανοίας ἔργα πράσσοντας
verses.king_james: But shewed first unto them of Damascus, and at Jerusalem, and throughout all the coasts of Judaea, and *then* to the Gentiles, that they should repent and turn to God, and do works meet for repentance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hac ex causa me Judæi, cum essem in templo, comprehensum tentabant interficere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕνεκα τούτων με οἵ ἰουδαῖοι συλλαβόμενοι ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ ἐπειρῶντο διαχειρίσασθαι
verses.king_james: For these causes the Jews caught me in the temple, and went about to kill *me*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Auxilio autem adjutus Dei usque in hodiernum diem, sto, testificans minori atque majori, nihil extra dicens quam ea quæ prophetæ locuti sunt futura esse, et Moyses,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπικουρίας οὖν τυχὼν τῆς παρὰ τοῦ θεοῦ ἄχρι τῆς ἡμέρας ταύτης ἕστηκα μαρτυρούμενος μικρῷ τε καὶ μεγάλῳ οὐδὲν ἐκτὸς λέγων ὧν τε οἱ προφῆται ἐλάλησαν μελλόντων γίνεσθαι καὶ μωσῆς,
verses.king_james: Having therefore obtained help of God, I continue unto this day, witnessing both to small and great, saying none other things than those which the prophets and Moses did say should come:

verses.latin_vulgate: si passibilis Christus, si primus ex resurrectione mortuorum, lumen annuntiaturus est populo et gentibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ παθητὸς ὁ χριστός εἰ πρῶτος ἐξ ἀναστάσεως νεκρῶν φῶς μέλλει καταγγέλλειν τῷ λαῷ καὶ τοῖς ἔθνεσιν
verses.king_james: That Christ should suffer, *and* that he should be the first that should rise from the dead, and should shew light unto the people, and to the Gentiles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc loquente eo, et rationem reddente, Festus magna voce dixit: Insanis, Paule: multæ te litteræ ad insaniam convertunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα δὲ αὐτοῦ ἀπολογουμένου ὁ φῆστος μεγάλῃ τῇ φωνῇ ἔφη μαίνῃ παῦλε τὰ πολλά σε γράμματα εἰς μανίαν περιτρέπει
verses.king_james: And as he thus spake for himself, Festus said with a loud voice, Paul, thou art beside thyself; much learning doth make thee mad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Paulus: Non insanio, inquit, optime Feste, sed veritatis et sobrietatis verba loquor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ οὐ μαίνομαι φησίν κράτιστε φῆστε ἀλλ᾽ ἀληθείας καὶ σωφροσύνης ῥήματα ἀποφθέγγομαι
verses.king_james: But he said, I am not mad, most noble Festus; but speak forth the words of truth and soberness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scit enim de his rex, ad quem et constanter loquor: latere enim eum nihil horum arbitror. Neque enim in angulo quidquam horum gestum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπίσταται γὰρ περὶ τούτων ὁ βασιλεύς πρὸς ὃν καὶ παῤῥησιαζόμενος λαλῶ λανθάνειν γὰρ αὐτὸν τι τούτων οὐ πείθομαι οὐδέν οὐ γάρ ἐστιν ἐν γωνίᾳ πεπραγμένον τοῦτο
verses.king_james: For the king knoweth of these things, before whom also I speak freely: for i am persuaded that none of these things are hidden from him; for this thing was not done in a corner.

verses.latin_vulgate: Credis, rex Agrippa, prophetis? Scio quia credis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πιστεύεις βασιλεῦ ἀγρίππα τοῖς προφήταις οἶδα ὅτι πιστεύεις
verses.king_james: King Agrippa, believest thou the prophets? I know that thou believest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Agrippa autem ad Paulum: In modico suades me christianum fieri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀγρίππας πρὸς τὸν παῦλον ἔφη, ἐν ὀλίγῳ με πείθεις χριστιανὸν γενέσθαι
verses.king_james: Then Agrippa said unto Paul, Almost thou persuadest me to be a Christian.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Paulus: Opto apud Deum, et in modico et in magno, non tantum te, sed etiam omnes qui audiunt hodie fieri tales, qualis et ego sum, exceptis vinculis his.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ παῦλος εἰπεν, εὐξαίμην ἂν τῷ θεῷ καὶ ἐν ὀλίγῳ καὶ ἐν πολλῷ οὐ μόνον σὲ ἀλλὰ καὶ πάντας τοὺς ἀκούοντάς μου σήμερον γενέσθαι τοιούτους ὁποῖος κἀγώ εἰμι παρεκτὸς τῶν δεσμῶν τούτων
verses.king_james: And Paul said, I would to God, that not only thou, but also all that hear me this day, were both almost, and altogether such as I am, except these bonds.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exsurrexit rex, et præses, et Bernice, et qui assidebant eis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταῦτα εἰπόντος αὐτοῦ, ἀνέστη ὁ βασιλεὺς καὶ ὁ ἡγεμὼν ἥ τε βερνίκη καὶ οἱ συγκαθήμενοι αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And when he had thus spoken, the king rose up, and the governor, and Bernice, and they that sat with them:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum secessissent, loquebantur ad invicem, dicentes: Quia nihil morte aut vinculis dignum quid fecit homo iste.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναχωρήσαντες ἐλάλουν πρὸς ἀλλήλους λέγοντες ὅτι οὐδὲν θανάτου ἄξιον ἢ δεσμῶν πράσσει ὁ ἄνθρωπος οὗτος
verses.king_james: And when they were gone aside, they talked between themselves, saying, This man doeth nothing worthy of death or of bonds.

verses.latin_vulgate: Agrippa autem Festo dixit: Dimitti poterat homo hic, si non appellasset Cæsarem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγρίππας δὲ τῷ φήστῳ ἔφη ἀπολελύσθαι ἐδύνατο ὁ ἄνθρωπος οὗτος εἰ μὴ ἐπεκέκλητο καίσαρα
verses.king_james: Then said Agrippa unto Festus, This man might have been set at liberty, if he had not appealed unto Caesar.
